description:
## Goal

`RefreshLoop::FiberTracker` exists with `track_spawn` /
`track_exit` / `stats` methods, but **no spawn site calls them**.
The counters never increment, so the periodic reporter
(`Monitoring.report_status`) reports `active=0, peak=0, spawns=0`
even on a long-running server with dozens of long-lived fibers.

## Why

The fiber spawn sites live in:
- src/services/app_bootstrap.cr (7 sites)
- src/services/memory_manager_actor.cr (1 site)
- src/infrastructure/actor.cr (1 site — the run_loop)
- src/controllers/admin_controller.cr (2 sites)
- src/quickheadlines.cr (4 sites)
- src/websocket/event_broadcaster.cr (1 site)
- src/websocket/socket_manager.cr (1 site — spawn_writer_fiber)
- src/fetcher/monitoring.cr (1 site)
- src/fetcher/feed_fetcher_concurrent.cr (2 sites)
- src/fetcher/refresh_supervisor.cr (3 sites)

That's ~23 spawn sites. Without wiring, we have **no visibility
into fiber accumulation** — if a spawn pattern leaks (e.g., the
per-connection writer fiber in `socket_manager.cr`), we won't
see it.

## Design

Option A (minimal): add `track_spawn` before each `spawn do` and
`track_exit` in an `ensure` block at the end of the spawned
block. ~46 line changes.

Option B (cleaner): introduce a helper
`RefreshLoop::FiberTracker.tracked_spawn(name, &block)` that does
the wiring in one place, then change each `spawn do` to use it.

Recommendation: **Option B**. One helper, then 23 call-site
changes (each is a one-line change to use the helper).

## Acceptance

- Every `spawn` in src/ uses `FiberTracker.tracked_spawn`
- After running the app for an hour, `Monitoring.report_status`
  reports non-zero `active` and `peak` (currently always 0)
- Build + spec + ameba clean
- Boot smoke test: server starts, fiber count > 0 in the
  reporter log
title: Wire FiberTracker into the actual spawn sites
